vulnerabilities.name: run-shell-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Using variable interpolation `${{...}}` with `github` context data in a `run:` step could allow an attacker to inject their own code into the runner. This would allow them to steal secrets and code. `github` context data can have arbitrary user input and should be treated as untrusted. Instead, use an intermediate environment variable with `env:` to store the data and use the environment variable in the `run:` script. Be sure to use double-quotes the environment variable, like this: "$ENVVAR".
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: express-sequelize-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Detected a sequelize statement that is tainted by user-input. This could lead to SQL injection if the variable is user-controlled and is not properly sanitized. In order to prevent SQL injection, it is recommended to use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: sql-injection-mysql-query-template
vulnerabilities.message: Template-literal SQL with interpolation. Use ?-placeholders and an array of values.

vulnerabilities.name: detect-replaceall-sanitization
vulnerabilities.message: Detected a call to `replaceAll()` in an attempt to HTML escape the string `tableData[i].description`. Manually sanitizing input through a manually built list can be circumvented in many situations, and it's better to use a well known sanitization library such as `sanitize-html` or `DOMPurify`.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: sql-injection-mysql-query-template
vulnerabilities.message: Template-literal SQL with interpolation. Use ?-placeholders and an array of values.

vulnerabilities.message: Template-literal SQL with interpolation. Use ?-placeholders and an array of values.

vulnerabilities.name: detected-generic-secret
vulnerabilities.message: Generic Secret detected

vulnerabilities.name: prototype-pollution-loop
vulnerabilities.message: Possibility of prototype polluting function detected. By adding or modifying attributes of an object prototype, it is possible to create attributes that exist on every object, or replace critical attributes with malicious ones. This can be problematic if the software depends on existence or non-existence of certain attributes, or uses pre-defined attributes of object prototype (such as hasOwnProperty, toString or valueOf). Possible mitigations might be: freezing the object prototype, using an object without prototypes (via Object.create(null) ), blocking modifications of attributes that resolve to object prototype, using Map instead of object.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

vulnerabilities.name: weak-password-hash-md5-js
vulnerabilities.message: MD5/SHA-1 used for password hashing. Use bcrypt (`bcrypt.hashSync(pw, 12)`) or argon2.

vulnerabilities.name: hardcoded-jwt-secret
vulnerabilities.message: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

vulnerabilities.name: hardcoded-hmac-key
vulnerabilities.message: Detected a hardcoded hmac key. Avoid hardcoding secrets and consider using an alternate option such as reading the secret from a config file or using an environment variable.

vulnerabilities.name: idor-find-by-id-no-ownership-js
vulnerabilities.message: Possible IDOR: model fetched/updated/deleted by ID from req.* without a visible ownership check. Verify that the current user is permitted to access this resource (e.g. add `where: { id, userId: req.user.id }` or an explicit policy check).

vulnerabilities.name: idor-find-by-id-no-ownership-js
vulnerabilities.message: Possible IDOR: model fetched/updated/deleted by ID from req.* without a visible ownership check. Verify that the current user is permitted to access this resource (e.g. add `where: { id, userId: req.user.id }` or an explicit policy check).

vulnerabilities.name: express-detect-notevil-usage
vulnerabilities.message: Detected usage of the `notevil` package, which is unmaintained and has vulnerabilities. Using any sort of `eval()` functionality can be very dangerous, but if you must, the `eval` package is an up to date alternative. Be sure that only trusted input reaches an `eval()` function.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-1104: Use of Unmaintained Third Party Components

vulnerabilities.name: idor-find-by-id-no-ownership-js
vulnerabilities.message: Possible IDOR: model fetched/updated/deleted by ID from req.* without a visible ownership check. Verify that the current user is permitted to access this resource (e.g. add `where: { id, userId: req.user.id }` or an explicit policy check).

vulnerabilities.name: eval-dynamic-string-js
vulnerabilities.message: eval() called with a non-literal expression. If the argument can be influenced by user input, this is a critical code injection vulnerability. Remove eval() or replace with JSON.parse() / a safe expression parser.

vulnerabilities.name: timing-attack-password-compare
vulnerabilities.message: Password/hash comparison using === is vulnerable to timing attacks. Use a constant-time comparison function like crypto.timingSafeEqual() or bcrypt.compare().

vulnerabilities.name: raw-html-format
vulnerabilities.message: User data flows into the host portion of this manually-constructed HTML. This can introduce a Cross-Site-Scripting (XSS) vulnerability if this comes from user-provided input. Consider using a sanitization library such as DOMPurify to sanitize the HTML within.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: remote-property-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Bracket object notation with user input is present, this might allow an attacker to access all properties of the object and even it's prototype. Use literal values for object properties.

vulnerabilities.name: idor-find-by-id-no-ownership-js
vulnerabilities.message: Possible IDOR: model fetched/updated/deleted by ID from req.* without a visible ownership check. Verify that the current user is permitted to access this resource (e.g. add `where: { id, userId: req.user.id }` or an explicit policy check).

vulnerabilities.name: express-res-sendfile
vulnerabilities.message: The application processes user-input, this is passed to res.sendFile which can allow an attacker to arbitrarily read files on the system through path traversal. It is recommended to perform input validation in addition to canonicalizing the path. This allows you to validate the path against the intended directory it should be accessing.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-73: External Control of File Name or Path

vulnerabilities.name: express-libxml-vm-noent
vulnerabilities.message: Detected use of parseXml() function with the `noent` field set to `true`. This can lead to an XML External Entities (XXE) attack if untrusted data is passed into it.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

vulnerabilities.name: express-res-sendfile
vulnerabilities.message: The application processes user-input, this is passed to res.sendFile which can allow an attacker to arbitrarily read files on the system through path traversal. It is recommended to perform input validation in addition to canonicalizing the path. This allows you to validate the path against the intended directory it should be accessing.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-73: External Control of File Name or Path

vulnerabilities.name: express-open-redirect
vulnerabilities.message: The application redirects to a URL specified by user-supplied input `query` that is not validated. This could redirect users to malicious locations. Consider using an allow-list approach to validate URLs, or warn users they are being redirected to a third-party website.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

vulnerabilities.name: sql-injection-mysql-query-template
vulnerabilities.message: Template-literal SQL with interpolation. Use ?-placeholders and an array of values.

vulnerabilities.name: mongodb-where-concat
vulnerabilities.message: MongoDB $where clause built with string concatenation — NoSQL injection risk. Use typed query operators instead of $where.

vulnerabilities.name: mongodb-where-template-literal
vulnerabilities.message: MongoDB $where clause uses a template literal — any interpolated variable enables arbitrary JavaScript injection into the MongoDB query engine. Replace $where with typed query operators ($eq, $gt, $lt) and validate inputs.

vulnerabilities.name: eval-dynamic-string-js
vulnerabilities.message: eval() called with a non-literal expression. If the argument can be influenced by user input, this is a critical code injection vulnerability. Remove eval() or replace with JSON.parse() / a safe expression parser.

vulnerabilities.name: timing-attack-password-compare
vulnerabilities.message: Password/hash comparison using === is vulnerable to timing attacks. Use a constant-time comparison function like crypto.timingSafeEqual() or bcrypt.compare().

vulnerabilities.name: unknown-value-with-script-tag
vulnerabilities.message: Cannot determine what 'subs' is and it is used with a '<script>' tag. This could be susceptible to cross-site scripting (XSS). Ensure 'subs' is not externally controlled, or sanitize this data.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: unknown-value-with-script-tag
vulnerabilities.message: Cannot determine what 'subs' is and it is used with a '<script>' tag. This could be susceptible to cross-site scripting (XSS). Ensure 'subs' is not externally controlled, or sanitize this data.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: unsafe-formatstring
vulnerabilities.message: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a util.format / console.log function. If an attacker injects a format specifier in the string, it will forge the log message. Try to use constant values for the format string.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

vulnerabilities.name: cors-wildcard
vulnerabilities.message: CORS wildcard origin (*) allows any website to make authenticated requests to your API. With credentials enabled, this is a critical vulnerability. Specify exact trusted origins: cors({ origin: "https://yourdomain.com" }).

vulnerabilities.name: express-check-directory-listing
vulnerabilities.message: Directory listing/indexing is enabled, which may lead to disclosure of sensitive directories and files. It is recommended to disable directory listing unless it is a public resource. If you need directory listing, ensure that sensitive files are inaccessible when querying the resource.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-548: Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing

vulnerabilities.name: template-explicit-unescape
vulnerabilities.message: Detected an explicit unescape in a Pug template, using either '!=' or '!{...}'. If external data can reach these locations, your application is exposed to a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability. If you must do this, ensure no external data can reach this location.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
